Title: A translation proofreader of archaeal origin imparts multi-aldehyde stress tolerance to land plants

Common_Genes.Source_Species: Arabidopsis thaliana; Pyrococcus horikoshii; Klebsormedium nitens
Common_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Arabidopsis thaliana; Pyrococcus horikoshii; Klebsormedium nitens
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native DTD2 promoter (~2.4 kb upstream region) for rescue; CaMV 35S constitutive promoter for overexpression
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: D-aminoacyl-tRNA deacylase 2 (DTD2), archaeal-type translation quality-control/chiral proofreading protein
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in 14-day-old Arabidopsis seedlings; strongly elevated in DTD2 overexpression line by qPCR
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Physiological carbon metabolism, energy generation, and signalling intermediates; toxic at elevated levels
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: DTD2 detoxifies physiologically abundant aldehyde stress by recycling aldehyde-modified D-aminoacyl-tRNAs, thereby improving Arabidopsis growth and germination under formaldehyde, methylglyoxal, and ethanol/acetaldehyde stress; overexpression enhances tolerance
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DTD2 from Arabidopsis efficiently deacylates formaldehyde-, acetaldehyde-, propionaldehyde-, and especially methylglyoxal-modified D-aa-tRNAs, but activity declines with longer-chain aldehyde adducts. 2. Arabidopsis dtd2 loss-of-function plants are hypersensitive to formaldehyde and methylglyoxal, and rescue with wild-type DTD2 restores tolerance whereas catalytic mutant H150A does not. 3. DTD2 overexpression under CaMV 35S increases seedling growth and root growth under aldehyde stress, supporting utility for multi-aldehyde stress tolerance engineering. 4. Archaeal and charophyte DTD2 homologs show similar activity, supporting archaeal origin and conservation during land-plant evolution.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA knockout mutant analysis; genomic complementation; catalytic mutant (H150A) rescue test; overexpression transgenics; in vitro deacylation assays; TLC; ESI-MS/MS; qPCR/RT-PCR; seedling growth and germination assays
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous formaldehyde; methylglyoxal; ethanol; D-tyrosine supplementation; 22°C growth on MS medium
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Bioinformatic KEGG/NCBI comparative analysis; no accession number reported
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious growth penalty reported for overexpression in this study; broader translational or metabolic effects not assessed
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising transgenic/engineering target to improve multi-aldehyde and likely multi-stress tolerance in crops; constitutive overexpression enhanced seedling and root growth under aldehyde stress; may be stacked with aldehyde-detoxification enzymes such as ALDH/glyoxalase pathways
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Core functional gene of the study. The paper frames DTD2 primarily as a translation-quality-control detoxifier rather than a classic metabolic enzyme or transcriptional regulator; therefore classified here as Common Gene. The source species and applied species must be distinguished: Arabidopsis DTD2 was functionally tested in Arabidopsis, whereas archaeal PhoDTD2 and charophyte KnDTD2 were biochemically tested as source homologs.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: D-aminoacyl-tRNA deacylase 1 (DTD1), bacterial-type chiral proofreading protein
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Arabidopsis DTD1 does not detoxify aldehyde-modified D-aa-tRNAs and is inactive on all tested aldehyde-modified D-aa-tRNA substrates
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtDTD1, the canonical plant D-chiral proofreader, is inactive on formaldehyde-, propionaldehyde-, methylglyoxal-, and butyraldehyde-modified D-aa-tRNAs. 2. The inactivity of DTD1 distinguishes DTD2 as the unique aldehyde-adduct proofreader in plants. 3. This functional difference supports the specialized role of archaeal-type DTD2 in aldehyde tolerance.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In vitro deacylation assays on aldehyde-modified D-Tyr-tRNATyr substrates
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro treatment with formaldehyde; propionaldehyde; methylglyoxal; butyraldehyde
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Limited value alone for aldehyde tolerance engineering because it lacks activity on aldehyde-modified D-aa-tRNAs
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although not the main positive gene target, DTD1 is directly tested in Results as a contrast core gene demonstrating specificity of DTD2.
